pattern: A number is a sum of distinct powers of three exactly when its base-3 representation uses only the digits 0 and 1.
hints: Distinct powers of three means each power is used at most once.
Think about what each digit of the number, written in base 3, would have to be.
Repeatedly take the remainder mod 3; if any remainder is 2, no valid representation exists.

edge_inputs.description: a single large power of three

stress.description: random values across the full range
constructed sums of distinct powers of three (should all be true)
